access_status.notes: Ruxience® (rituximab-pvvr) and Riabni® (rituximab-arrx) are preferred rituximab products. Patient must have a contraindication, intolerance, or failure of Ruxience® and Riabni® prior to the consideration of Rituxan Hycela.

covered_indications.condition: Extranodal Marginal Zone Lymphoma (EMZL) of the Stomach & Nongastric Sites (Noncutaneous)
Histologic Transformation of Indolent Lymphomas to Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma
Kaposi-sarcoma associated herpesvirus (KSHV)-Associated Inflammatory Cytokine Syndrome

covered_indications.criteria: Patient has nodular lymphocyte-predominant disease.

criteria: Patient must have a contraindication, intolerance, or failure of Ruxience® (rituximab-pvvr) and Riabni® (rituximab-arrx) prior to the consideration of another rituximab product.
Patient is at least 18 years of age.
Patient has received at least one full dose of a rituximab product by intravenous infusion prior to initiating therapy.
Patient does not have a severe, active infection.

Patient has been screened for the presence of hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection (i.e., HBsAg and anti-HBc) prior to initiating therapy and patients with evidence of current or prior HBV infection will be monitored for HBV reactivation during treatment.
Patient is CD20 antigen expression positive.
Rituxan Hycela will not be used with intravenous chemotherapy agents or intravenous radioimmunotherapy.
Provider will confirm that patient has not received a live vaccine within 28 days prior to starting treatment and live vaccines will not be administered concurrently while on treatment.
For Adult Hodgkin Lymphoma: Patient has nodular lymphocyte-predominant disease.

Renewal Criteria: Patient continues to meet the universal and other indication-specific relevant criteria such as concomitant therapy requirements (not including prerequisite therapy), performance status, etc. identified in section III.
Renewal Criteria: Duration of authorization has not been exceeded (refer to section I).

Renewal Criteria: Absence of unacceptable toxicity from the drug. Examples of unacceptable toxicity include: severe hypersensitivity or other administration reactions (e.g., local cutaneous reactions), tumor lysis syndrome (TLS), severe mucocutaneous reactions (e.g., paraneoplastic pemphigus, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, lichenoid dermatitis, etc.), progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML), hepatitis B virus reactivation, serious infections (bacterial, fungal or viral), cardiac adverse reactions, renal toxicity, bowel obstruction and perforation, etc.
Renewal Criteria: Disease response with treatment as defined by stabilization of disease or decrease in size of tumor or tumor spread.

steps.notes: Patient must have a contraindication, intolerance, or failure of the required drug(s).

restrictions: Must be administered by a healthcare provider.

payer_name: Priority Health Commercial and MyPriority Plans

access_status.notes: Covered Alternative(s): Q5123 - Riabni (rituximab-arrx), Q5119 - Ruxience (rituximab-pvvr), Q5115 - Truxima (rituximab-abbs). Search our Medical Drug List to understand the clinical requirements for the alternatives. Some drugs may require the provider to request authorization prior to the drug being administered.

steps.notes: Preferred alternative. May require prior authorization. Site of Service restrictions apply.
Preferred alternative. May require prior authorization. Site of Service restrictions apply.
Preferred alternative. May require prior authorization. Site of Service restrictions apply.

covered_indications.condition: Antineutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibody (ANCA)-Associated Vasculitis, Initial Treatment
covered_indications.criteria: Patient has an ANCA-associated vasculitis (e.g., granulomatosis with polyangiitis (Wegener’s granulomatosis), or microscopic polyangiitis) AND The medication is being administered in combination with glucocorticoids

covered_indications.condition: Antineutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibody (ANCA)-Associated Vasculitis, Follow-Up Treatment
covered_indications.criteria: Patient achieved disease control with induction treatment AND If the patient previously received a course of therapy, at least 16 weeks will elapse between courses
Therapy is initiated in combination with a corticosteroid unless contraindicated
Subsequent infusions will be administered no sooner than 16 weeks following the previous infusion of a rituximab product
Subsequent infusions will be administered no sooner than 16 weeks following the previous infusion of a rituximab product

Patient has tried ONE conventional synthetic disease-modifying antirheumatic drug (DMARD) for at least 3 months (Exception: if the patient already has a 3-month trial of at least one biologic other than the requested drug, a biosimilar of the requested biologic does not count; a patient who has already tried a biologic is not required to 'step back' and try a conventional synthetic DMARD) AND The medication will not be used concurrently with another biologic or with a targeted synthetic DMARD

covered_indications.condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis, After one course of Rituximab product
covered_indications.criteria: 16 weeks or greater will elapse between treatment courses AND The medication will not be used concurrently with another biologic or with a targeted synthetic DMARD

covered_indications.condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis, After two or more courses of Rituximab product
covered_indications.criteria: 16 weeks or greater will elapse between treatment courses AND The medication will not be used concurrently with another biologic or with a targeted synthetic DMARD AND (Patient experienced a beneficial clinical response when assessed by at least one objective measure OR Patient experienced an improvement in at least one symptom, such as decreased joint pain, morning stiffness, or fatigue; improved function or activities of daily living; decreased soft tissue swelling in joints or tendon sheaths)
Patient has chronic graft-versus-host disease AND Patient has tried at least one systemic medication for graft-versus-host disease

covered_indications.condition: Graft-Versus-Host Disease, After previous course of Rituximab product
covered_indications.criteria: (When assessed by at least one objective measure, patient experienced a beneficial clinical response from baseline (prior to initiating a rituximab product) OR Compared with baseline (prior to initiating a rituximab product), patient experienced an improvement in at least one symptom, such as improvement in skin, oral mucosal, ocular, or gastrointestinal symptoms (e.g., nausea, vomiting, anorexia))
The medication will be used as part of a conditioning regimen for allogeneic transplant
Patient has tried one other therapy

covered_indications.condition: Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP), After previous course of Rituximab product
covered_indications.criteria: At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses AND According to the prescriber, the patient responded to therapy AND According to the prescriber, the patient has relapsed

covered_indications.condition: Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors, Initial Therapy
covered_indications.criteria: According to the prescriber, patient developed an immunotherapy-related toxicity AND Patient developed this immunotherapy-related toxicity while receiving a checkpoint inhibitor AND Patient is symptomatic despite a trial of at least ONE systemic corticosteroid

covered_indications.condition: Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors, After previous course of Rituximab product
Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease, Initial Therapy
covered_indications.criteria: Patient is ≥ 18 years of age AND Diagnosis is confirmed by high-resolution computed tomography

covered_indications.condition: Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease, After previous course of Rituximab product
covered_indications.criteria: 24 weeks or greater will elapse between treatment courses AND Patient has experienced a beneficial response to therapy with rituximab
According to the prescriber, the patient is at moderate risk or high risk for the progressive loss of kidney function

covered_indications.condition: Membranous Nephropathy, After previous course of Rituximab product

covered_indications.criteria: The medication is being used for frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent disease

covered_indications.condition: Minimal Change Disease, After previous course of Rituximab product

covered_indications.criteria: According to the prescriber, the patient has experienced inadequate efficacy or significant intolerance to at least TWO other disease-modifying agents for multiple sclerosis AND Medication will not be used concurrently with another disease-modifying agent used for multiple sclerosis AND At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses

covered_indications.condition: Multiple Sclerosis, Currently receiving Rituximab for < 1 year
covered_indications.criteria: Medication will not be used concurrently with another disease-modifying agent used for multiple sclerosis AND At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses

covered_indications.condition: Multiple Sclerosis, Currently receiving Rituximab for 1 year or more
covered_indications.criteria: Medication will not be used concurrently with another disease-modifying agent used for multiple sclerosis AND At least 6 months will elapse between treatment courses AND (Patient experienced a beneficial clinical response when assessed by at least one objective measure OR Patient experienced stabilization, slow progression, or improvement in at least one symptom such as motor function, fatigue, vision, bowel/bladder function, spasticity, walking/gait, or pain/numbness/tingling sensation)

Patient has confirmed anti-muscle-specific tyrosine kinase antibody-positive myasthenia gravis AND (Patient previously received or is currently receiving pyridostigmine OR Patient has had inadequate efficacy, contraindication, or significant intolerance to pyridostigmine) AND Patient has tried at least one immunosuppressant therapy AND Patient has evidence of unresolved symptoms of myasthenia gravis

covered_indications.condition: Myasthenia Gravis, After previous course of Rituximab product
covered_indications.criteria: According to the prescriber, patient is continuing to derive benefit from the rituximab product
Patient is ≤ 18 years of age AND (Patient has tried at least one systemic corticosteroid OR Patient has tried at least one glucocorticoid-sparing agent for nephrotic syndrome)

covered_indications.condition: Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome, After previous course of Rituximab product

covered_indications.criteria: The medication will be used for desensitization therapy prior to or immediately after transplantation OR The medication will be used for antibody-mediated rejection
Patient has tried at least ONE standard immunomodulating or immunosuppressant agent

covered_indications.condition: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) [Lupus], After previous course of Rituximab product
covered_indications.criteria: 6 months or greater will elapse between treatment courses
The medication will be used in combination with systemic corticosteroids AND The medication will be used in combination with therapeutic plasma exchange

criteria: Initial approval requires rituximab to be prescribed by or in consultation with a physician who specializes in the condition being treated (rheumatologist, nephrologist, pulmonologist, immunologist, dermatologist, hematologist, oncologist, neurologist, or a physician affiliated with a transplant center, as appropriate for the condition).
Documentation is required where noted in the criteria as [documentation required].
Preferred product criteria is met for the product(s) as listed in the below table(s).

Patient cannot continue to use each formulary alternative (Truxima, Riabni, and Ruxience) due to a formulation difference in the inactive ingredient(s) (e.g., differences in stabilizing agent, buffering agent, and/or surfactant) which, according to the prescriber, would result in a significant allergy or serious adverse reaction [documentation required].

steps.notes: Must be tried and patient cannot continue due to a formulation difference in inactive ingredient(s) resulting in significant allergy or serious adverse reaction [documentation required].
Must be tried and patient cannot continue due to a formulation difference in inactive ingredient(s) resulting in significant allergy or serious adverse reaction [documentation required].
Must be tried and patient cannot continue due to a formulation difference in inactive ingredient(s) resulting in significant allergy or serious adverse reaction [documentation required].

limits.value: Initial Therapy for ANCA-Associated Vasculitis: 375 mg/m2 per dose intravenously for 4 doses separated by at least 7 days OR Up to two 1,000 mg intravenous doses separated by at least 2 weeks.
Follow-Up Treatment for ANCA-Associated Vasculitis (≥ 18 years of age): Up to 1,000 mg intravenously for 6 doses.
Follow-Up Treatment for ANCA-Associated Vasculitis (< 18 years of age): Up to 250 mg/m2 intravenously for two doses.
Initial Treatment or Treatment of a Relapse for Pemphigus Vulgaris: One course of therapy, which consists of up to two 1,000 mg doses intravenously separated by at least 2 weeks.
Maintenance Therapy for Pemphigus Vulgaris: Up to 500 mg per dose intravenously every 6 months.
Rheumatoid Arthritis: One course of therapy, which consists of up to two 1,000 mg intravenous doses separated by at least 2 weeks.

Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia: One course of therapy (4 doses) consisting of 375 mg/m2 intravenously with doses separated by at least 7 days OR 100 mg intravenously with doses separated by at least 7 days.
Graft-Versus-Host Disease: Up to 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously with doses separated by at least 7 days.
Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation: One dose of 375 mg/m2 intravenously before transplant and three doses of 1,000 mg/m2 intravenously separated by at least 7 days after transplant.
Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP): Up to 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously with doses separated by at least 7 days.

Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors: Up to 500 mg/m2 or up to 1,000 mg administered intravenously for 2 doses separated by at least 14 days OR Up to 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously for 4 doses separated by at least 7 days.
Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease: One course of therapy, which consists of up to two 1,000 mg intravenous doses separated by at least 2 weeks.
Membranous Nephropathy: 1,000 mg administered intravenously for 2 doses separated by at least 14 days OR 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously for up to 4 doses separated by at least 7 days.
Minimal Change Disease: 1,000 mg administered intravenously for up to 2 doses separated by at least 14 days OR 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously for up to 4 doses separated by at least 7 days.
Multiple Sclerosis: Up to 2,000 mg (total) administered as one or two intravenous infusions administered over 1 month.

Myasthenia Gravis: 1,000 mg administered intravenously for 2 doses separated by at least 14 days OR 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously for 4 doses separated by at least 7 days with or without an additional 375 mg/m2 administered monthly for 2 doses (up to 6 doses total) OR 750 mg/m2 administered intravenously for 2 doses separated by at least 14 days.

Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder: Up to 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously for 4 doses separated by at least 7 days OR Up to two 1,000 mg doses administered as an intravenous infusion separated by at least 2 weeks.
Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome: 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously for up to 4 doses separated by at least 7 days.
Solid Organ Transplantation: Requested dosage is based on a transplant center’s protocol.
Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) [Lupus]: Approve the requested dose.
Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura: Up to 375 mg/m2 administered intravenously for up to 4 doses separated by at least 7 days.

policy_metadata.policy_name: Rituximab Intravenous Products for Non-Oncology Indications

access_status.notes: Members already on Rituxan or other rituximab products will be required to change therapy to Riabni, Ruxience, or Truxima unless they meet specific criteria.

covered_indications.criteria: For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of immune thrombocytopenic purpura (ITP); and Documented platelet count < 30 x 10^9 / L; and History of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to one of the following: Anti-D immunoglobulin, Corticosteroids, Immune globulin, Thrombopoietin receptor agonist (TPO-RA) [e.g., eltrombopag, Nplate (romiplostim)], Splenectomy
For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of moderate to severe pemphigus vulgaris

covered_indications.condition: Wegener’s granulomatosis or microscopic polyangiitis (both ANCA-associated vasculidities)
covered_indications.criteria: For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of Wegener’s granulomatosis or microscopic polyangiitis; and One of the following: Patient is receiving concurrent therapy with glucocorticoids, History of contraindication or intolerance to glucocorticoids
For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of autoimmune hemolytic anemia

For initial therapy, all of the following: Moderate to severe disease activity (e.g., swollen, tender joints with limited range of motion); and One of the following: Patient is receiving concurrent therapy with methotrexate, History of contraindication or intolerance to methotrexate; and History of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to at least one tumor necrosis factor (TNF) inhibitors [e.g., adalimumab, Enbrel (etanercept), infliximab, Cimzia (certolizumab), Simponi (golimumab)]; and Patient is not receiving rituximab in combination with a targeted immunomodulator. [e.g., adalimumab, Enbrel (etanercept), Cimzia (certolizumab), Simponi (golimumab), Orencia (abatacept), Xeljanz (tofacitinib), Olumiant (baricitinib), Rinvoq (upadacitinib)]
For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of PTLD

For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (NMOSD) by a neurologist confirming all of the following: Serologic testing for anti-aquaporin-4 immunoglobulin G (AQP4-IgG)/NMO-IgG antibodies has been performed; and Past medical history of (if AQP4-IgG/NMO-IgG positive one of the following, if negative two of the following): Optic neuritis, Acute myelitis, Area postrema syndrome: episode of otherwise unexplained hiccups or nausea and vomiting, Acute brainstem syndrome, Symptomatic narcolepsy or acute diencephalic clinical syndrome with NMOSD-typical diencephalic MRI lesions, Symptomatic cerebral syndrome with NMOSD-typical brain lesions; and Diagnosis of multiple sclerosis or other diagnoses have been ruled out; and Prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist; and Patient is not receiving rituximab in combination with any of the following: Disease modifying therapies for the treatment of multiple sclerosis [e.g., Gilenya (fingolimod), Tecfidera (dimethyl fumarate), Ocrevus (ocrelizumab), etc.], Anti-IL6 therapy [e.g., Actemra (tocilizumab), Kevzara (sarilumab)], Complement inhibitors [e.g., Soliris (eculizumab), Ultomiris (ravulizumab)]

For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of immunotherapy-related encephalitis; and Recent immunotherapy treatment with a checkpoint inhibitor [e.g., Keytruda (pembrolizumab), Opdivo (nivolumab), Tecentriq (atezolizumab)]; and One of the following: Patient has had limited or no improvement after treatment with glucocorticoids for a minimum of 7 days; or History of contraindication or intolerance to glucocorticoids; or Both of the following: Patient is positive for autoimmune encephalopathy antibody; and Infectious causes (e.g., viral) of encephalitis have been ruled out

For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura; and Used in combination with plasma exchange therapy; and One of the following: Patient is receiving concurrent therapy with glucocorticoids, History of contraindication or intolerance to glucocorticoids

For initial therapy, all of the following: One of the following: Diagnosis of primary progressive multiple sclerosis (PPMS), Diagnosis of relapsing forms of MS (e.g., relapsing-remitting MS, secondary-progressive MS with relapses, progressive-relapsing MS with relapses); and Patient is not receiving rituximab in combination with any of the following: Disease modifying therapy (e.g., interferon beta preparations, dimethyl fumarate, glatiramer acetate, natalizumab, fingolimod, cladribine, siponimod, or teriflunomide), B cell targeted therapy (e.g., ocrelizumab, belimumab, ofatumumab), Lymphocyte trafficking blockers (e.g., alemtuzumab, mitoxantrone)

For initial therapy, all of the following: Diagnosis of Immunoglobulin G4-related disease (IgG4-RD); and Confirmation of IgG4-RD by a positive assessment using the ACR/EULAR classification criteria, demonstrated by all of the following: Involvement of at least 1 or more organ(s) in a manner consistent with IgG4-RD; and Exclusion criteria is negative and consistent with an IgG4-RD diagnosis (e.g., clinical findings, serologic results, radiology assessments, pathology interpretations); and Inclusion criteria is positive and signifies a diagnosis of IgG4-RD (e.g., clinical findings, serologic results, radiology assessments, pathology interpretations); and Prescribed by, or in consultation with, a specialist with expertise in the treatment of IgG4-RD; and Patient is not receiving rituximab in combination with a disease modifying therapy for the treatment of IgG4-related disease [e.g., Uplizna (inebilizumab-cdon)]

criteria: For initial therapy, prescriber attests dosing is in accordance with the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved labeling; or for indications without FDA approved dosing, prescriber attests there is published clinical evidence to support the dosing.
For continuation of therapy, documentation of a positive clinical response.

For continuation of therapy, prescriber attests dosing is in accordance with the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved labeling; or for indications without FDA approved dosing, prescriber attests there is published clinical evidence to support the dosing.

steps.notes: Patient must have a history of intolerance, contraindication, or serious adverse event to Riabni, Ruxience, or Truxima; and Physician attests that, in their clinical opinion, the same intolerance, contraindication, or serious adverse event would not be expected to occur with Rituxan.

limits.value: 12 months
limits.notes: Initial authorization will be for no more than 12 months.

limits.value: 12 months
limits.notes: Reauthorization will be for no more than 12 months.
